label: section1_queryB

messages_in.content: What's the typical Young's modulus of mild steel?

final_message:
The typical Young‚Äôs modulus (also known as the modulus of elasticity) of mild steel is approximately **200 GPa** (gigapascals), which is equivalent to **29,000 ksi** (kilopounds per square inch), or **29 x 10^6 psi** (pounds per square inch).

This value can vary slightly depending on the exact composition and processing of the steel, but 200 GPa (or 29,000 ksi) is commonly used in engineering calculations for structural steel.

The typical Young‚Äôs modulus (also known as the modulus of elasticity) of mild steel is approximately **200 GPa** (gigapascals), which is equivalent to **29,000 ksi** (kilopounds per square inch), or **29 x 10^6 psi** (pounds per square inch).
